 {"id":"bd-1oj9","title":"Implement pi.registerProvider() for custom LLM providers","description":"Phase 1: Allow extensions to register custom LLM providers with standard APIs.

## API Spec (Phase 1 - Basic Registration)
```typescript
pi.registerProvider(name, {
  baseUrl: string,
  apiKey: string,  // env var name
  api: 'anthropic-messages' | 'openai-responses',
  models: Model[]
});
```

## Scope (Phase 1 Only)
This task implements basic provider registration using standard API types.
Advanced features are in separate phases:
- Phase 2 (bd-39q8): Custom streamSimple handler
- Phase 3 (bd-3my9): OAuth support

## Implementation Requirements
1. ProviderRegistry in extension runtime
2. Hostcall handler for 'registerProvider'
3. Integration with provider system (src/provider.rs)
4. Integration with model registry (src/models.rs)
5. Validation of api type (anthropic-messages | openai-responses)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: registerProvider stores provider config
- Unit test: invalid api type rejected
- Integration test: registered provider appears in --list-models
- E2E test: pi --provider custom-provider uses registered provider

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (ProviderRegistry)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handler)
- src/provider.rs (integrate extension providers)
- src/models.rs (add extension provider models)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:14.120496236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T01:37:11.224790805Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oj9","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:14.128100916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1oj9","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:13:34.441437607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Establish a **profile-driven optimization loop** for the PiJS runtime and connectors with reproducible outputs.

# Method (Extreme Optimization)
1) Baseline: `hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10` on key extension workloads.
2) Profile: `cargo flamegraph` for CPU, heaptrack for allocations.
3) Prove: golden outputs + isomorphism checklist for every change.
4) Implement: only opportunities with score ≥ 2.0.
5) Verify: sha256 of golden outputs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Benchmark scripts for representative extensions.
- Opportunity matrix with impact/confidence/effort.
- Initial flamegraph and top hotspots.
- Log artifacts per run (JSONL + summary table).

# Output
- Short performance report tied to `bd-btq` success criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1pf","title":"Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming","description":"# Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming

## Goal
Create infrastructure to record and replay real API responses for provider testing.

## Background
VCR (Video Cassette Recorder) testing pattern:
1. First run: Make real API call, record response to file
2. Subsequent runs: Replay recorded response, no network

This gives us:
- Real response format testing (not simplified mocks)
- Deterministic CI (no flaky network tests)
- Real error response testing (rate limits, auth failures)

## Implementation

### Recording Mode
```rust
// tests/vcr/mod.rs

pub struct VcrRecorder {
    cassette_path: PathBuf,
    mode: VcrMode,
}

pub enum VcrMode {
    Record,    // Make real calls, save to cassette
    Playback,  // Replay from cassette, fail if not found
    Auto,      // Playback if exists, else record
}

impl VcrRecorder {
    pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> Self { ... }

    pub async fn request(&self, req: Request) -> Response {
        match self.mode {
            VcrMode::Record => self.record_and_return(req).await,
            VcrMode::Playback => self.playback(req),
            VcrMode::Auto => ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Cassette Format
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "test_name": "anthropic_streaming_text",
  "recorded_at": "2026-02-02T...",
  "interactions": [
    {
      "request": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        "headers": { ... },
        "body": { ... }
      },
      "response": {
        "status": 200,
        "headers": { ... },
        "body_chunks": [
          "event: message_start\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          "event: content_block_delta\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          ...
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### SSE Streaming Support
For streaming responses, store individual chunks:
- Each SSE event as separate chunk
- Preserve timing information (optional replay delay)
- Support partial responses (stream interruption)

### Sensitive Data Redaction
```rust
pub fn redact_cassette(cassette: &mut Cassette) {
    // Replace API keys with placeholder
    cassette.redact_header("x-api-key", "[REDACTED]");
    cassette.redact_header("authorization", "[REDACTED]");

    // Replace sensitive response data
    cassette.redact_body_field("api_key", "[REDACTED]");
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) to log record/playback actions.
- Log cassette path, mode, request summary (method/url), and redaction summary.
- On playback mismatch, log expected vs actual interaction keys + diff hints.

## Files to Create
- tests/vcr/mod.rs - VCR infrastructure
- tests/vcr/cassette.rs - Cassette format/loading
- tests/vcr/recorder.rs - Recording logic
- tests/vcr/playback.rs - Playback logic
- tests/fixtures/vcr/ - Directory for cassette files

## Testing the VCR System
- Unit test: Load cassette from JSON
- Unit test: Match request to interaction
- Unit test: Redaction works
- Integration: Record + playback cycle

## Environment Variables
- VCR_MODE=record|playback|auto
- VCR_CASSETTE_DIR=path/to/cassettes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VCR infrastructure compiles
- [ ] Recording mode saves cassettes
- [ ] Playback mode replays cassettes
- [ ] SSE streaming supported
- [ ] Sensitive data redacted
- [ ] Record/playback actions logged with detail
- [ ] CI uses playback mode only

 {"id":"bd-1q1p","title":"Fix: --theme validation should happen before --version early exit","description":"Bug: when running `pi --theme nonexistent.json --version`, the --version flag causes an early return before theme validation, so invalid themes are silently ignored.

## Expected Behavior
Invalid --theme should error even when combined with --version.

## Current Behavior (before fix)
--version short-circuits on line 83-86 of main.rs, theme validation is never reached.

## Fix Applied
- Moved early theme validation for path-like specs before --version check
- Made `looks_like_theme_path` public in theme.rs
- Named themes (without .json, /, ~) still validated later after resource loading

## Status
Fix is complete but codebase has 56 unrelated compile errors from other agents' work. Fix will pass tests once those are resolved.

## Test
`cargo test e2e_cli_theme` - all 4 tests should pass after codebase compiles","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-05T02:30:28.081870763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:37:19.459347331Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:37:19.459284925Z","close_reason":"Fix committed - theme path validation now happens before --version check","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":241,"issue_id":"bd-1q1p","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PearlTower picking this up now: validate --theme before --version early exit in src/main.rs, then run cargo test e2e_cli_theme_flag_invalid_path and full quality gates.","created_at":"2026-02-05T02:33:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1r2f","title":"Task: Implement Session hostcall dispatch","description":"# Task: Implement Session Hostcall Dispatch

## Objective

Implement the Session hostcall handler that routes pi.session.* calls from JS to the Rust SessionContext.

## Background

Extensions call pi.session methods which become Session hostcalls. The dispatch handler must:
1. Receive the hostcall from JS
2. Route to appropriate SessionContext method
3. Serialize result back to JS

## Hostcall Protocol

### Request Format
```json
{
    "type": "session",
    "method": "getMessages" | "getLastNMessages" | "appendMessage" | "fork",
    "args": { ... }
}
```

### Methods

1. **getMessages**: No args, returns message array
2. **getLastNMessages**: { n: number }, returns message array
3. **appendMessage**: { message: Message }, returns void
4. **fork**: { entryId: string }, returns session ID
5. **getId**: No args, returns session ID string
6. **getCwd**: No args, returns cwd string
7. **getStartedAt**: No args, returns ISO timestamp

## Implementation

### Dispatch Handler

```rust
impl HostcallDispatcher {
    async fn dispatch_session(
        &self,
        method: &str,
        args: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> Result<HostcallResponse> {
        let session = self.session_context.as_ref()
            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow\!("No session context bound"))?;
        
        match method {
            "getId" => {
                let session = session.read().await;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(session.id())))
            }
            
            "getCwd" => {
                let session = session.read().await;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(session.cwd().to_string_lossy())))
            }
            
            "getStartedAt" => {
                let session = session.read().await;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(session.started_at().to_rfc3339())))
            }
            
            "getMessages" => {
                let session = session.read().await;
                let messages = session.get_messages();
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(messages)))
            }
            
            "getLastNMessages" => {
                let n = args.get("n")
                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
                    .ok_or_else(|| anyhow\!("Missing 'n' parameter"))? as usize;
                
                let session = session.read().await;
                let messages = session.get_last_n_messages(n);
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(messages)))
            }
            
            "appendMessage" => {
                let message: Message = serde_json::from_value(
                    args.get("message").cloned()
                        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow\!("Missing 'message' parameter"))?
                )?;
                
                let mut session = session.write().await;
                session.append_message(message)?;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(null)))
            }
            
            "fork" => {
                let entry_id = args.get("entryId")
                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
                    .ok_or_else(|| anyhow\!("Missing 'entryId' parameter"))?;
                
                let session = session.read().await;
                let new_id = session.fork(entry_id).await?;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(new_id)))
            }
            
            _ => Err(anyhow\!("Unknown session method: {}", method)),
        }
    }
}
```

### JS Bridge (pi.session)

Update PI_BRIDGE_JS to expose session methods:

```javascript
pi.session = {
    get id() {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getId', args: {} });
    },
    
    get cwd() {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getCwd', args: {} });
    },
    
    get startedAt() {
        const iso = __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getStartedAt', args: {} });
        return new Date(iso);
    },
    
    getMessages() {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getMessages', args: {} });
    },
    
    getLastNMessages(n) {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getLastNMessages', args: { n } });
    },
    
    appendMessage(message) {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'appendMessage', args: { message } });
    },
    
    async fork(entryId) {
        return await __pi_hostcall({ type: 'session', method: 'fork', args: { entryId } });
    },
};
```

### Binding Session to Runtime

In agent initialization:

```rust
// Bind session to extension runtime
let session_context = create_session_context(session);
runtime.bind_session(session_context);

// Later, session hostcalls can access it
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: getId returns session ID
2. Unit test: getCwd returns working directory
3. Unit test: getMessages returns history
4. Unit test: getLastNMessages returns subset
5. Unit test: appendMessage adds to history
6. Unit test: fork creates new session
7. Integration test: Full flow from JS to Rust

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-2i56 (Session implements SessionContext)
- Depends on: bd-2d99 (HostcallDispatcher)
- Part of: bd-3o8s (Session Context Binding feature)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All session hostcall methods dispatched
- [ ] Session bound to runtime correctly
- [ ] Properties (id, cwd, startedAt) work
- [ ] Methods (getMessages, appendMessage, fork) work
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:05:17.848075490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:05.366015971Z","deleted_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:05.366010511Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"Merged into existing beads (bd-1tyz, bd-1u4t)","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-36mc","title":"Fix interactive TUI newline drift (raw mode requires CRLF)","description":"# Goal
Fix interactive TUI newline drift in raw mode by aligning CR/LF handling and cursor/viewport calculations.

# Symptoms / Repro
- In raw mode, Enter/Shift+Enter can produce visible drift (cursor offsets, missing/extra blank lines).
- Drift accumulates after multi-line input, paste, or window resize.

# Scope
- Normalize incoming newline sequences for rendering (CRLF in raw mode) while keeping stored text as \n.
- Ensure editor cursor, viewport offsets, and wrapping logic use a single newline representation.
- Apply the same normalization to paste paths, slash-command inserts, and programmatic editor changes.
- Verify compatibility with both text and bash input modes.

# Tests
- Add state/snapshot coverage for multi-line input, paste, and resize with raw mode enabled.
- Regression test for Enter/Shift+Enter cursor positioning and viewport alignment.
- Log before/after cursor position, rendered lines, and input buffer in TestLogger artifacts.

# Acceptance Criteria
- No newline drift after repeated inserts and resizes in raw mode.
- Tests are deterministic and emit JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9.

 {"id":"bd-37a","title":"Implement theme file format and loader","description":"# Implement theme file format and loader

## Goal
Define theme JSON schema and implement theme file loading.

## Implementation

### Theme Types (src/theme.rs)
```rust
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct Theme {
    pub name: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub colors: ThemeColors,
    pub syntax: SyntaxColors,
    pub ui: UiColors,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct ThemeColors {
    pub foreground: String,
    pub background: String,
    pub accent: String,
    pub success: String,
    pub warning: String,
    pub error: String,
    pub muted: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct SyntaxColors {
    pub keyword: String,
    pub string: String,
    pub number: String,
    pub comment: String,
    pub function: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct UiColors {
    pub border: String,
    pub selection: String,
    pub cursor: String,
}
```

### Theme Discovery
```rust
impl Theme {
    pub fn discover_themes() -> Vec<PathBuf> {
        let mut paths = Vec::new();
        
        // Global themes
        if let Some(home) = dirs::home_dir() {
            let global = home.join(".pi/agent/themes");
            if global.exists() {
                paths.extend(glob_json(&global));
            }
        }
        
        // Project themes
        let project = PathBuf::from(".pi/themes");
        if project.exists() {
            paths.extend(glob_json(&project));
        }
        
        paths
    }
    
    pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;
        let theme: Theme = serde_json::from_str(&content)?;
        theme.validate()?;
        Ok(theme)
    }
    
    pub fn load_by_name(name: &str) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        // Search discovered themes for matching name
        for path in Self::discover_themes() {
            if let Ok(theme) = Self::load(&path) {
                if theme.name == name {
                    return Ok(theme);
                }
            }
        }
        Err(Error::ThemeNotFound(name.to_string()))
    }
}
```

### Validation
```rust
impl Theme {
    fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), Error> {
        // Validate all color strings are valid hex
        self.validate_color(&self.colors.foreground)?;
        self.validate_color(&self.colors.background)?;
        // ... etc
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn validate_color(s: &str) -> Result<(), Error> {
        if !s.starts_with('#') || s.len() != 7 {
            return Err(Error::InvalidColor(s.to_string()));
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Default Themes
```rust
impl Theme {
    pub fn dark() -> Self {
        Self {
            name: "dark".into(),
            version: "1.0".into(),
            colors: ThemeColors {
                foreground: "#d4d4d4".into(),
                background: "#1e1e1e".into(),
                accent: "#007acc".into(),
                // ...
            },
            // ...
        }
    }
    
    pub fn light() -> Self {
        // Light theme defaults
    }
}
```

## Testing
- Test: Load valid theme JSON
- Test: Reject invalid JSON
- Test: Reject invalid colors
- Test: Discover themes from directories
- Test: Default themes valid

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Theme struct defined
- [ ] JSON loading works
- [ ] Validation catches bad colors
- [ ] Discovery finds themes
- [ ] Default themes exist","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-37l","title":"Migrate Anthropic provider to asupersync HTTP","description":"# Migrate Anthropic provider to asupersync HTTP

## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/anthropic.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
Anthropic is the most complex provider implementation:
- Extended thinking support (streaming thinking deltas)
- Cache control headers (ephemeral caching)
- Tool use with JSON schema
- Multiple content block types in responses
- 748 lines of code, 2 unit tests currently

Current reqwest usage (lines to change):
- Line ~200: reqwest::Client creation
- Line ~250: client.post(url).json(&request)
- Line ~280: response.bytes_stream() for SSE
- Various header handling

## Implementation Steps

1. **Replace Client Creation**
```rust
// Before (reqwest)
let client = reqwest::Client::new();

// After (asupersync)
use crate::http::Client;
let client = Client::new()?;
```

2. **Replace Request Building**
```rust
// Before
let response = client
    .post(&url)
    .header("x-api-key", &api_key)
    .header("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
    .json(&request)
    .send()
    .await?;

// After
let response = client
    .post(&url)
    .header("x-api-key", &api_key)
    .header("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
    .json(&request)
    .send(&cx)
    .await?;
```

3. **Replace SSE Streaming**
```rust
// Before (reqwest-eventsource or manual)
let mut stream = response.bytes_stream();
while let Some(chunk) = stream.next().await { ... }

// After (asupersync SSE adapter)
use crate::http::SseStream;
let mut sse = SseStream::new(response);
while let Some(event) = sse.next(&cx).await {
    match event? { ... }
}
```

4. **Update Function Signatures**
```rust
// Add Cx parameter to stream()
async fn stream(
    &self,
    cx: &Cx,  // New parameter
    context: &Context,
    options: &StreamOptions,
) -> Result<...>
```

## Anthropic-Specific Considerations
1. **Extended Thinking**: Must handle thinking_start/delta/end events correctly
2. **Cache Headers**: anthropic-beta header for cache control
3. **Error Responses**: Parse error JSON from non-2xx responses
4. **Rate Limiting**: Respect 429 responses with retry-after header
5. **Content Types**: text, thinking, tool_call blocks in single response

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper)
- bd-pwz (SSE streaming adapter)

## Testing
- Existing 2 unit tests must still pass
- Add integration test with mock SSE server
- Test extended thinking stream parsing
- Test tool call accumulation

## Files
- src/providers/anthropic.rs (modify ~50 lines)
- src/provider.rs (update trait if Cx needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reqwest imports in anthropic.rs
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Streaming works end-to-end
- [ ] Extended thinking parsing works
- [ ] Tool calls parsed correctly
- [ ] Error handling preserved","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CodexGPT52","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:48.745145157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:56.027518307Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:33.922326594Z","close_reason":"Already migrated to asupersync HTTP + SSE","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37l","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-37l","depends_on_id":"bd-pwz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-37l","author":"CodexGPT52","text":"Starting verification/closure: codebase already uses asupersync HTTP client (src/http/client.rs) + canonical SSE (src/sse.rs) in src/providers/anthropic.rs; will confirm and close + unblock bd-1iy/bd-1vo/bd-ivj.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:20:27Z"},{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-37l","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verified bd-37l is already complete:

-{"id":"bd-37qz","title":"Feature: Hostcall Dispatcher Infrastructure","description":"# Feature: Hostcall Dispatcher Infrastructure

## Overview

This is the CRITICAL PATH for extensions parity. The hostcall dispatcher is the bridge between JavaScript extension code and Rust implementation. Without this, no extension functionality works.

## Current State

**What exists (src/extensions_js.rs):**
- `HostcallKind` enum with 6 variants: Tool, Exec, Http, Session, Ui, Events
- `HostcallRequest` struct with call_id, kind, params
- `HostcallOutcome` enum: Success(Value), Error { code, message }
- `drain_hostcall_requests()` method on PiJsRuntime
- `complete_hostcall(call_id, outcome)` method to resolve JS Promises

**What's missing:**
- Consumer that calls drain_hostcall_requests() in the agent loop
- Dispatcher that routes each HostcallKind to appropriate handler
- Result mapping from handler output to HostcallOutcome
- Integration with agent.rs run loop

## Technical Design

```rust
// In agent.rs or new src/extension_dispatcher.rs

pub struct ExtensionDispatcher {
    runtime: Arc<PiJsRuntime>,
    tool_registry: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
    http_connector: HttpConnector,
    session: Arc<dyn ExtensionSession>,
    ui_handler: Box<dyn ExtensionUiHandler>,
}

impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// Process all pending hostcalls. Call after each JS tick.
    pub async fn process_pending(&self) -> Result<()> {
        let requests = self.runtime.drain_hostcall_requests();
        
        for req in requests {
            let outcome = match req.kind {
                HostcallKind::Tool { name, input } => {
                    self.dispatch_tool(&name, input).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Exec { cmd, args, opts } => {
                    self.dispatch_exec(&cmd, &args, opts).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Http { request } => {
                    self.dispatch_http(request).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Session { op, args } => {
                    self.dispatch_session(&op, args).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Ui { method, params } => {
                    self.dispatch_ui(&method, params).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Events { op, args } => {
                    self.dispatch_events(&op, args).await
                }
            };
            
            self.runtime.complete_hostcall(&req.call_id, outcome)?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

## Integration Points

1. **Agent Loop (src/agent.rs)**
   - After calling `runtime.tick()`, call `dispatcher.process_pending()`
   - Handle async execution without blocking the main loop
   - Respect cancellation tokens

2. **Tool Registry**
   - Must be accessible from dispatcher
   - Extension-registered tools added to registry

3. **Session State**
   - ExtensionSession implementation needs actual session reference

## Risks & Mitigations

**Risk**: Blocking the agent loop during long-running hostcalls
**Mitigation**: Use async dispatch, respect timeouts from HostcallRequest

**Risk**: Promise resolution after JS context destroyed
**Mitigation**: Track call_ids, ignore completions for dead contexts

## Subtasks

This feature breaks down into:
1. Create ExtensionDispatcher struct and trait
2. Implement tool dispatch handler
3. Implement exec dispatch handler  
4. Implement http dispatch handler
5. Implement session dispatch handler
6. Implement ui dispatch handler
7. Implement events dispatch handler
8. Wire dispatcher into agent loop
9. Add timeout and cancellation handling
10. Add conformance tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] drain_hostcall_requests() called after each JS tick
- [ ] All 6 hostcall kinds properly routed
- [ ] Outcomes flow back via complete_hostcall()
- [ ] JS Promises resolve/reject correctly
- [ ] Benchmarks show <100µs dispatch overhead
- [ ] No memory leaks on long-running sessions","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:51:58.504472329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:27:31.651907647Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37qz","depends_on_id":"bd-30zg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:51:58.510930534Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":239,"issue_id":"bd-37qz","author":"WhiteHeron","text":"Triaging current build break in hostcall dispatcher (reported E0277 at src/extension_dispatcher.rs:325 + src/extensions.rs:4185). Plan: inspect shared uncommitted diffs (tool/exec/http/session dispatch), fix compile/clippy/test, then commit+push to keep main green. Coordinating with reservation holders via Agent Mail.","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:27:31Z"}]}

## Overview

This is the CRITICAL PATH for extensions parity. The hostcall dispatcher is the bridge between JavaScript extension code and Rust implementation. Without this, no extension functionality works.

## Current State

**What exists (src/extensions_js.rs):**
- `HostcallKind` enum with 6 variants: Tool, Exec, Http, Session, Ui, Events
- `HostcallRequest` struct with call_id, kind, params
- `HostcallOutcome` enum: Success(Value), Error { code, message }
- `drain_hostcall_requests()` method on PiJsRuntime
- `complete_hostcall(call_id, outcome)` method to resolve JS Promises

**What's missing:**
- Consumer that calls drain_hostcall_requests() in the agent loop
- Dispatcher that routes each HostcallKind to appropriate handler
- Result mapping from handler output to HostcallOutcome
- Integration with agent.rs run loop

## Technical Design

```rust
// In agent.rs or new src/extension_dispatcher.rs

pub struct ExtensionDispatcher {
    runtime: Arc<PiJsRuntime>,
    tool_registry: Arc<ToolRegistry>,
    http_connector: HttpConnector,
    session: Arc<dyn ExtensionSession>,
    ui_handler: Box<dyn ExtensionUiHandler>,
}

impl ExtensionDispatcher {
    /// Process all pending hostcalls. Call after each JS tick.
    pub async fn process_pending(&self) -> Result<()> {
        let requests = self.runtime.drain_hostcall_requests();
        
        for req in requests {
            let outcome = match req.kind {
                HostcallKind::Tool { name, input } => {
                    self.dispatch_tool(&name, input).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Exec { cmd, args, opts } => {
                    self.dispatch_exec(&cmd, &args, opts).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Http { request } => {
                    self.dispatch_http(request).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Session { op, args } => {
                    self.dispatch_session(&op, args).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Ui { method, params } => {
                    self.dispatch_ui(&method, params).await
                }
                HostcallKind::Events { op, args } => {
                    self.dispatch_events(&op, args).await
                }
            };
            
            self.runtime.complete_hostcall(&req.call_id, outcome)?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

## Integration Points

1. **Agent Loop (src/agent.rs)**
   - After calling `runtime.tick()`, call `dispatcher.process_pending()`
   - Handle async execution without blocking the main loop
   - Respect cancellation tokens

2. **Tool Registry**

   - Extension-registered tools added to registry

3. **Session State**

**Mitigation**: Use async dispatch, respect timeouts from HostcallRequest

**Risk**: Promise resolution after JS context destroyed

- [ ] JS Promises resolve/reject correctly
- [ ] Benchmarks show <100µs dispatch overhead

 {"id":"bd-37z","title":"Spec: Hostcall ABI + capability manifest","description":"# Goal
Define a **precise hostcall ABI** and **capability manifest** so every extension I/O is explicit, auditable, and enforceable.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Request/response schema: `id`, `capability`, `method`, `params`, `timeout_ms`, `cancel_token`.
- Error taxonomy: deterministic mapping for timeouts, denied capability, IO failure.
- Streaming contract: chunked responses + backpressure signaling.
- Capability manifest schema: declarative list of required connectors + scope (paths, hosts, methods).
- Structured log schema: request_id, extension_id, capability, scope, duration, outcome, redaction flags.

# Security Invariants
- No hostcall executes without a granted capability.
- Capability scope is checked before any connector side effect.
- Logs are append-only and redact secrets by default.

# Tests This Enables
- ABI conformance fixtures (unit).
- Capability-deny tests with exact error mapping.
- E2E log assertions using the standardized schema.

# Non-goals
- Implicit global OS access.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:21:53.288452067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:48.887495033Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:04:41.049085160Z","close_reason":"Completed (spec+schema+Rust validation)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-37z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting bd-37z. I’ll spec host_call/host_result ABI + capability manifest by updating EXTENSIONS.md (authoritative narrative) and tightening docs/schema/extension_protocol.json for host_call (capability+method+params+timeout+cancel) + host_result (typed error taxonomy + optional streaming fields). Will update src/extensions.rs sample messages/tests accordingly and run cargo check/clippy/fmt/test.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:32:33Z"},{"id":177,"issue_id":"bd-37z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed bd-37z: Spec: Hostcall ABI + capability manifest

- EXTENSIONS.md: added Hostcall ABI (capability+method+params+timeout_ms+cancel_token), capability derivation rules, error taxonomy, streaming contract, capability manifest schema (pi.ext.cap.v1), and hostcall evidence ledger (params_hash + timing) via pi.ext.log.v1.
- docs/schema/extension_protocol.json: host_call now requires capability/method/params (+ timeout_ms/cancel_token/context); host_result adds typed error + chunk/backpressure metadata and enforces error presence iff is_error=true; added host_call_error_code/error and host_stream_chunk/backpressure defs.
- src/extensions.rs: updated protocol structs + validation; enforces declared capability matches core-derived requirement; validates params/output are objects; validates capability manifest schema; removed panic! macros from tests (UBS).
- benches/extensions.rs + tests/extensions_manifest.rs updated for new host_call ABI and required_capability_for_host_call signature.

Verified: cargo fmt/check/clippy -D warnings/test all pass; ubs --diff --only=rust shows Critical=0.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:04:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-385a","title":"Interactive: represent tool calls/results as first-class chat items","description":"# Goal
Refactor interactive conversation rendering so tool calls and tool results are modeled as distinct message/item types.

  - user message
  - assistant message
  - assistant thinking block(s)
  - tool call start (name + args)
  - tool result (stdout/stderr/exit + metadata)
  - system notices
- Ensure items can be rendered with consistent styling.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tool calls/results are no longer “just text”; they are structured items.

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+cd "$ROOT"
+
+if ! command -v hyperfine >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  echo "hyperfine not installed; install with: cargo install hyperfine" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+cargo build --release --bin pijs_workload
+
+BIN="$ROOT/target/release/pijs_workload"
+ITERATIONS="${ITERATIONS:-200}"
+TOOL_CALLS="${TOOL_CALLS:-1}"
+
+hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10 "$BIN --iterations ${ITERATIONS} --tool-calls ${TOOL_CALLS}"
